cards.back: Gas is the unit of measurement for the amount of computational work required to execute transactions and smart contracts on the Ethereum network.
A block is a collection of transactions that are verified and added to the Ethereum blockchain in a specific order.
A transaction is a message sent on the Ethereum network, which can be a simple transaction or a more complex smart contract execution.
A smart contract is a self-executing program stored and replicated on the Ethereum blockchain, which automates the enforcement of an agreement or the execution of a specific task.
The gas price is the amount of Ether that a user is willing to pay for each unit of computational work required to execute a transaction or smart contract on the Ethereum network.
